title: Classifying Angles: Acute, Right, and Obtuse

steps.instruction: There are three main types of angles based on their measurement.
steps.work: Acute: less than 90°. Right: exactly 90°. Obtuse: between 90° and 180°.
steps.explanation: The key benchmark is 90°. Angles are classified by how they compare to 90°.

steps.instruction: An angle measures 120°. Compare it to 90°.
steps.work: 120° > 90° and 120° < 180°. It is an obtuse angle.
steps.explanation: Since 120 is greater than 90 but less than 180, the angle opens wider than a right angle.
Acute angles are 'sharp' — they are smaller than the corner of a square.

title: Finding a Missing Angle on a Straight Line

steps.instruction: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°.
steps.explanation: A straight line forms a 180° angle. Any angles that make up that line must sum to 180°.

steps.instruction: If Angle A = 72°, find Angle B.
steps.explanation: We substitute the known angle and solve for the unknown.
The missing angle is 108°, which is obtuse.
